method: F001 build.py (fixture generator) copied verbatim into two sibling run dirs, patched identically (FIXTURE_REPO retargeted to current session; AUDIT_MD_VERSION updated to sha256:c0fca070; RUN_ID/STARTED_AT/FINISHED_AT/REPORT_DATE set to identical values across both copies — the 'identical seed' condition). Each copy executed independently as a separate python3 process. Emitted .audit/ trees compared via `diff -rq` plus per-file sha256.
conclusion: F001 fixture generator is pipeline-deterministic under identical inputs (AUDIT_MD_VERSION, RUN_ID, timestamps, seed). No latent nondeterminism in dict iteration, hash randomization, floating-point ordering, or path traversal (the pipeline uses sorted() at every emission boundary). Validates the §DETERMINISM contract's bit-stability promise at the fixture layer. NOTE: this is a fixture-level T-sweep (pure-python generator); a model-level T-sweep (same AUDIT.md invocation against a live model with temperature=0) is a separate, stronger check and remains a v5+ candidate when model-execution credentials are wired in.
